reasoning: The base rate for a NATO Article 5 invocation is extremely low, having occurred only once in the alliance's 75-year history (following September 11, 2001). With only about 50 days remaining in the forecast period (Nov 9 to Dec 31, 2025), a base-rate prediction would be well under 1%. While the war in Ukraine brings heightened risk and Poland reportedly invoked Article 4 in September 2025 following a Russian drone incursion, NATO allies have explicitly maintained that accidental or spillover drone/missile incursions do not constitute an intentional armed attack on the alliance, and thus do not trigger Article 5. Statements from NATO officials and geopolitical analysts through October 2025 reaffirm that neither NATO nor Russia wants a direct military confrontation. Geopolitical realities, lack of any large-scale direct attack on a NATO member, and the very short timeframe remaining in 2025 make an Article 5 invocation highly improbable. I am assigning a 5% probability (the minimum allowed by instructions) to account for tail risks of rapid, unexpected escalation.
